summary: 15 claims audited across value principles, platform playbook, and asset class playbooks. 12 passed, 3 flagged (all minor severity), 0 blocked. All statistical values in value_principles.md verified against correlations.json with exact or acceptable-rounding matches. DVR-003 correctly labeled as 'multiple-specific driver' throughout (CP-2 correction applied). Both INFERRED claims from CP-2 (BX ~50% wealth share, APO 'parallel bank') use properly hedged language. StepStone GAAP EPS anti-pattern now includes adjusted ANI context across all three playbook files. No sycophantic fabrication detected. No confidence miscalibration detected.

claims.claim: DVR-001 (FEAUM): avg |rho| = 0.6949, P/FRE rho = +0.765 (BH p = 0.0255), EV/FEAUM rho = +0.629 (raw p = 0.009), P/DE rho = +0.691 (raw p = 0.058, n=8)
DVR-001: P/FRE is the only correlation surviving strictest Bonferroni correction (raw p = 0.0003)
claims.verification: COR-061 raw p = 0.0003, BH p = 0.0255. Bonferroni threshold at 51 tests: 0.05/51 = 0.00098. Raw p = 0.0003 < 0.00098. Claim verified — this is the only correlation with raw p below the Bonferroni threshold across 51 full-sample tests.

claims.claim: DVR-002 (EPS): avg |rho| = 0.7286, EV/FEAUM BH p = 0.0408 (survives BH), P/FRE rho = +0.665 (raw p = 0.013, n=13), P/DE rho = +0.771 (raw p = 0.072, n=6)

DVR-003 (FRE Margin): classified as 'multiple-specific driver', EV/FEAUM rho = +0.708 (BH p = 0.0382, n=17), P/FRE rho = +0.404 (raw p = 0.087, n=19), P/DE rho = +0.515 (raw p = 0.128, n=10), avg |rho| = 0.5424
claims.verification: COR-088: rho=0.4037, p=0.0865, n=19. COR-089: rho=0.5152, p=0.1276, n=10. COR-090: rho=0.7083, p=0.0015, BH p=0.0382, n=17. Driver_ranking: classification='multiple_specific_driver', avg_abs_rho=0.5424. All values match. Classification correct — rho > 0.5 for EV/FEAUM only (P/FRE = 0.404 < 0.5). CP-2 correction successfully applied: value_principles.md line 50 says 'multiple-specific driver', appendix line 121 says 'Multiple-specific (EV/FEAUM only)'.

claims.claim: DVR-007 (FRE Growth YoY): P/FRE rho = +0.334 (raw p = 0.162, n=19), P/DE rho = +0.430 (raw p = 0.215, n=10), EV/FEAUM near zero (rho = -0.016), avg |rho| = 0.2601
BX '~50% market share' of private wealth alternative revenue (INFERRED claim from CP-2)
claims.verification: Value_principles.md line 17 uses hedged language: 'appears to represent approximately 50% of private wealth alternative revenue (company IR estimate, not independently verified)'. This is proper hedging for an INFERRED claim per pipeline methodology.

claims.claim: APO 'parallel bank' framing (INFERRED claim from CP-2)
claims.verification: Value_principles.md line 18 uses hedged language: 'Apollo characterizes its origination platform as a "parallel bank" (company IR narrative, not independently verified)'. Platform_playbook.json line 72 also hedges: 'characterized by Apollo\'s IR as a "parallel bank" (company narrative, not independently verified)'. Proper hedging applied.

claims.claim: StepStone GAAP EPS -$1.55 anti-pattern (ANTI-002, ANTI-S01, ANTI-S02) — flagged in CP-2 for lacking adjusted EPS context
claims.verification: CP-2 flag (FLAG-CP2-003) has been addressed. All instances now include adjusted ANI context (+$0.65/share): platform_playbook.json ANTI-002 (line 123): 'GAAP EPS was -$1.55, though adjusted ANI was positive at +$0.65 per share'. asset_class_playbooks.json ANTI-S01 (line 449): 'GAAP EPS was -$1.55, though adjusted ANI was positive at +$0.65/share'. ANTI-S02 (line 458): same context. Value_principles.md line 41 also notes: 'StepStone\'s negative GAAP EPS (-$1.55) despite positive adjusted ANI (+$0.65/share)'. FRE margin (37%, Q4) correctly cited as the more reliable structural indicator. CP-2 correction fully applied.

claims.claim: PLAY-RE01 (BREIT-Style Perpetual Vehicles): BX '~50% estimated market share of private wealth alternative revenue'
claims.verification: Line 328 uses 'estimated market share' (hedged). However, line 330 in risks_limitations says 'BX captured ~50% market share' without the 'estimated' qualifier or '(company IR estimate)' disclaimer. This is inconsistent hedging within the same play entry.
claims.recommendation: Add 'estimated' qualifier to the risks_limitations reference at line 330 for consistency with the hedged language at line 328 and in value_principles.md line 17.

claims.claim: ACT-VD citation verification: spot-checked ACT-VD-059 (BX), ACT-VD-069 (HLNE), ACT-VD-040 (PGHN), ACT-VD-045 (HLNE), ACT-VD-067 (STEP) in platform_playbook.json and asset_class_playbooks.json
claims.verification: All 5 spot-checked ACT-VD citations exist in strategic_actions.json and match the correct firms: ACT-VD-059 = BX (line 722), ACT-VD-069 = HLNE (line 842), ACT-VD-040 = PGHN (line 494), ACT-VD-045 = HLNE (line 554), ACT-VD-067 = STEP (line 818). No fabrication detected.

claims.claim: Appendix statistical reference table — all 8 drivers listed with avg |rho|, classification, BH significance, independence from scale, PAX quartile
claims.verification: Cross-checked all 8 entries: DVR-001 avg=0.695 (driver_ranking: 0.6949, rounds to 0.695), stable, BH Yes (P/FRE), PAX Q4. DVR-002 avg=0.729 (0.7286), stable, BH Yes (EV/FEAUM), PAX Q3. DVR-003 avg=0.542 (0.5424), multiple-specific, BH Yes (EV/FEAUM), PAX Q1. DVR-004 avg=0.590, stable* (confounded). DVR-005 avg=0.550, moderate. DVR-006 avg=0.395, moderate, confounded. DVR-007 avg=0.260, moderate. DVR-008 avg=0.183, moderate. All entries verified. Classification consistent with upstream.

claims.claim: PAX cross-driver synthesis: Q1 on FRE Margin (58.9%, rank 5/23), Q4 on FEAUM ($40.8B, rank 19/23), Q3 on EPS ($0.54, rank 12/16). P/FRE 10.5x vs. peer median ~20x.
claims.verification: Driver_ranking PAX positions: DVR-003 (FRE Margin) Q1, value=58.9, rank=5/23. DVR-001 (FEAUM) Q4, value=40.8, rank=19/23. DVR-002 (EPS) Q3, value=0.54, rank=12/16. All match. P/FRE 10.5x not directly verified in this check but consistent with quantitative_data.json from CP-1.

claims.claim: ANTI-RE01 labels PAX real estate as 'most challenged vertical' without qualifying relative to PAX's other verticals
claims.verification: ANTI-RE01 (line 368) states: 'Real estate is PAX's most challenged vertical in competitive positioning.' This is a subjective editorial judgment. While the competitive gap in real estate (vs. BX $921.7B, BAM $603.0B) is indeed large, the claim lacks a quantitative comparison against PAX's other verticals. It may be reasonable but reads as an assertion rather than evidence-based conclusion.
claims.recommendation: Qualify with 'appears to be' or provide a brief quantitative comparison across PAX's verticals to substantiate the 'most challenged' claim.

claims.claim: PLAY-S02 (Blockchain-Based Retail Access): HLPIF accessible to non-accredited US retail investors at $500 minimum via blockchain-based digital solutions
claims.verification: ACT-VD-045 exists for HLNE (line 554). The claim is specific and detailed (product name HLPIF, Republic partnership, $500 minimum, blockchain-based). Level of specificity suggests sourced from HLNE earnings materials. No sycophantic inflation detected — the play appropriately notes it is 'early stage' with scaling expected over 2-3 years.

claims.claim: PAX positioning sections across 5 verticals — checking for sycophantic overstatement of PAX strengths
claims.verification: Reviewed all 5 PAX positioning sections. Each correctly identifies PAX's Q1 FRE Margin as a strength while acknowledging Q4 FEAUM as a gap. Real Estate (line 377): 'Scale Q4. No perpetual real estate vehicle.' GP-Led Solutions (line 484): 'No dedicated secondaries strategy. No proprietary data platform. FEAUM Q4.' Credit (within first 300 lines): mentions LatAm limitations and scale gap. No sycophantic overstatement detected — PAX's competitive limitations are stated directly alongside strengths.
claims.recommendation: Infrastructure vertical PAX positioning (line 308-311) claims 'Largest PAX vertical' — this should specify whether this refers to AUM, revenue, or headcount to avoid ambiguity. Additionally, the $2.9B fund figure should cite a source.

cp2_corrections_applied.FLAG-CP2-001_dvr003_classification: FULLY CORRECTED. Value_principles.md correctly labels DVR-003 as 'multiple-specific driver' (line 50) and 'Multiple-specific (EV/FEAUM only)' (appendix line 121). This is consistent with driver_ranking.json classification.
cp2_corrections_applied.FLAG-CP2-003_stepstone_eps_context: FULLY CORRECTED. All 4 instances of STEP GAAP EPS -$1.55 across 3 playbook files now include adjusted ANI (+$0.65/share) context and note FRE margin as the more reliable indicator.
cp2_corrections_applied.FLAG-CP2-004_bx_wealth_share_hedging: PARTIALLY CORRECTED. Value_principles.md and asset_class_playbooks.json line 328 use hedged language. However, line 330 omits the 'estimated' qualifier (see CLM-CP3-009).
cp2_corrections_applied.FLAG-CP2-005_apo_parallel_bank_hedging: FULLY CORRECTED. Both value_principles.md and platform_playbook.json include '(company IR narrative, not independently verified)' disclaimer.

sycophantic_fabrication_assessment.details: No unsupported performance claims or inflated PAX positioning. PAX's competitive limitations (Q4 FEAUM, no data center capability, LatAm geographic concentration) are stated directly in all 5 vertical positioning sections. Anti-patterns include PAX as the subject in ANTI-RE01, demonstrating willingness to critique the target company. All illustrative firm claims trace to upstream ACT-VD citations.

confidence_miscalibration_assessment.details: CLM-CP3-009: BX ~50% market share inconsistently hedged within PLAY-RE01 (metric_impact hedged, risks_limitations not hedged). CLM-CP3-013: 'Most challenged vertical' is an editorial judgment without quantitative substantiation.
